concerns.text: Task T004 contains hallucinated text: 'The research question is to determine the relationship between input complexity and system response time... (Author et al., 2023).' This contradicts the Spec's FR-001 and the project's actual goal (molecular complexity vs. chemical properties). Including this in the config file or comments violates the Single Source of Truth and introduces a semantic constraint violation by defining the wrong research objective.

Tasks T005 and T014 explicitly override Spec FR-001 (PubChem CID 1-5000) to use the HuggingFace dataset 'sagawa/pubchem-10m-canonicalized'. While the Plan.md documents this deviation, the Spec's Success Criteria (SC-001, SC-002) and Assumptions still reference the CID 1-5000 scope. The tasks implement a scope change without a corresponding update to the Spec's acceptance criteria, creating a semantic gap where the deliverable (random sample) does not match the Spec's defined acceptance test (CID 1-5000 range).

Task T039 implements 'stratified subsampling' if the dataset exceeds 4GB to satisfy SC-006. However, SC-003 requires 95% confidence intervals derived from the *entire* valid dataset to ensure stability. Subsampling the data for bootstrap violates the semantic intent of SC-003 (stability of the full sample) and SC-006 (memory constraint) by introducing a new source of variance not accounted for in the Spec's success criteria. The task relaxes the statistical rigor required by the Spec.

Task T004 description ends with a sentence fragment: 'The requirement specifies that the system must handle at least one CID..' This appears to be a copy-paste error or incomplete thought, confusing the data volume requirements.
